key_findings: ["Zn/Mg stoichiometry controls the balance between strength and corrosion resistance in dilute Al-Zn-Mg alloys.", "An alloy with Zn/Mg ratio of 3.68 exhibited a corrosion sensitivity index of 3.6% and grain boundary precipitate size of 15.3 µm, attributed to coarse and discontinuous grain boundary precipitates.", "Decreasing Zn/Mg ratio significantly increases strength, SCC resistance, and electrochemical corrosion resistance in Al-Zn-Mg alloys with Zn+Mg ≈ 7 wt%.", "Addition of 0.04 wt% Ce to Al-Zn-Mg alloy significantly reduces self-corrosive current density and increases passivation film resistance, lowering stress corrosion sensitivity index from 0.612 to 0.219."]
optimization_goals: ["Balance between strength and corrosion resistance", "Improve corrosion resistance while maintaining mechanical properties"]

No specific composition percentages (e.g., exact Zn, Mg, Al wt%) are provided for a single alloy, only ratios and trends.
